title: Section I. Notice of Funding Opportunity Description
generation_instructions: Write a comprehensive overview of the funding opportunity, including: 1. The background and rationale for inner ear imaging research; 2. The scope of research supported, including specific imaging techniques; 3. The clinical relevance and potential impact of the research; 4. Specific requirements for clinical trials supported by this NOFO; 5. Clear description of what is not responsive to the NOFO; 6. Emphasis on multidisciplinary team approach; 7. Include specific examples of how improved imaging can benefit clinical management.
search_queries: in vivo high-resolution inner ear imaging
clinical applications of inner ear imaging
advanced imaging for auditory and vestibular structures
inner ear imaging probes and contrast agents

title: Research Plan Section
generation_instructions: Develop a detailed research plan section that: 1. Clearly articulates the research aims and objectives; 2. Provides a detailed description of the proposed methodology, including imaging techniques and data analysis; 3. Presents a realistic timeline and plan for translating the proposed work into a clinical setting; 4. Specifies the current imaging resolution, and details how the proposed improvements will advance current techniques and capabilities; 5. Addresses the feasibility of the proposed approach, including potential challenges; 6. Explains the clinical settings and conditions under which the techniques can be used, and how it will inform clinical care and decision-making, and 7. Discusses what auditory and vestibular pathologies can be better diagnosed and what treatments may become feasible if the increase in imaging resolution is achieved.
search_queries: high-resolution in vivo imaging experimental design
clinical translation of advanced imaging techniques
inner ear imaging data analysis methods
feasibility of novel imaging for human inner ear
timeline for translating research to clinic setting
methods for improving inner ear imaging resolution
inner ear pathologies diagnosed by high-resolution imaging
